location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in San Francisco office

secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager with growth and consumer-craft focus

level_signals: 8+ years of experience in product management
Comp range $200K-$300K base, consistent with senior IC level
Expected to shape product roadmap and product team itself
Described as highly strategic and execution-oriented with outsized role on lean team

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 8+ years of experience in product management, with a track record of owning and shipping meaningful product areas in B2B SaaS, enterprise software, AI products, consumer products, or high-engagement workflow platforms.
You have a strong product sense for translating complex, ambiguous problems into clear, polished product experiences, and you are comfortable moving between strategy and detailed execution.
You have a deep interest in AI and software, and you are excited by the opportunity to shape consumer-grade experiences that combine search, chat, context, and proactive intelligence.
You have a strong track record of driving growth, engagement, and retention through experimentation, instrumentation, and data-driven iteration.
You are an outstanding cross-functional partner and influencer who can drive outcomes across surfaces you don't directly own, aligning adjacent teams into a cohesive user experience.

You have strong technical fluency and are comfortable partnering with engineering and data teams on systems involving retrieval, ranking, client infrastructure, and intelligence layers across web, desktop, mobile, and SDK surfaces.

You can demonstrate granular product thinking, including how a user should experience a workflow, what the right interaction model is, and how to sequence product capabilities into something customers will actually adopt.
You are comfortable operating in highly ambiguous spaces and can create clarity, momentum, and alignment even when the product area is still emerging.
You are an excellent written and verbal communicator, and have a learning and growth mindset.
You have a proven track record of taking ownership, taking initiative, and delivering results, and collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners.
You are an early adopter in building with or adopting AI for your own product craft and you are excited about helping customers accelerate their own AI adoption journey.

scope.team_size_estimate: lean product team (exact size not stated)
scope.surface: Homepage, Mobile, Desktop, Browser Extension, SDK, app shell, navigation, notifications, settings
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / Work AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: User retention and daily active usage
core_responsibilities: Define and drive product strategy and roadmap for Core Experience
Own end-to-end quality of Glean's core surfaces including Homepage, Mobile, Desktop, Extension, and SDK
Partner closely with Search and Chat teams to influence their roadmaps and ensure cohesive user journey
Spend significant time with users, customers, and stakeholders to understand friction and drop-off
Drive growth, engagement, virality, collaboration, and session depth
Champion rapid experimentation using A/B tests, instrumentation, and qualitative research
Partner with engineering, design, and data teams to turn ambiguous concepts into well-scoped products
Drive customer-focused prioritization and efficient execution
Collaborate with go-to-market, solutions, support, and forward-deployed teams
Bring high bar for product craft including concrete workflows, UI behavior, and adoption paths

compensation.equity_form: equity eligible (certain roles); form not specified

green_flags: Wide base salary range disclosed ($200K-$300K), competitive for senior PM roles
Clear product surface ownership — Homepage, Mobile, Desktop, Extension, SDK
Outsized influence on lean team: shapes both roadmap and team itself
Well-articulated company strategy (chat-first AI entry point) giving PM clear north star
Comprehensive benefits including home office stipend, education and wellness stipends, 401k
red_flags: 4 days per week in San Francisco office is a near-onsite requirement, eliminating remote and most hybrid candidates
Very wide base salary range ($200K-$300K) gives little signal about target level
8+ year minimum is a hard bar with no stated exceptions or equivalent paths
High buzzword density in JD may indicate evolving scope or unclear product boundaries
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not stated — unclear for international candidates
